compiled_state.title: As a developer, reviewers signal when a diff reveals a fundamentally flawed approach
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add approach_viability_concern signal that reviewers emit when they detect a pattern of hallucinations or fragile fixes in the same diff, allowing the orchestrator to route back to implementation-plan.
**Why**: Fragile fixes and hallucinations in a diff can indicate the implementation approach itself is wrong — incremental patches waste cycles and degrade code quality.
**Scope**:
- IN: Signal definition in reviewer output, reviewer instructions for when to emit, orchestrator reads signal, mid-resolution handling
- OUT: Implementation-plan re-invocation routing (depends on f2be-1da6).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, reviewers emit an approach_viability_concern signal when they detect a pattern of hallucinations or fragile fixes in the same diff
  ← Satisfies: "When a reviewer detects a pattern of hallucinations or fragile fixes in the same diff, it emits an approach_viability_concern signal"
- When this story is complete, the orchestrator reads the approach_viability_concern signal from the review output and can use it to route back to implementation-plan
  ← Satisfies: "that the orchestrator can use to route back to implementation-plan"
- When this story is complete, if approach_viability_concern is emitted during the resolution loop, the orchestrator completes the current resolution attempt then checks the signal before the next attempt
  ← Satisfies: "approach_viability_concern signal that the orchestrator can use"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Adversarial] validate-review-output.sh enforces exactly 3 top-level keys (scores, findings, summary) plus optional_top set. approach_viability_concern must either be added to the optional_top set or embedded in summary text (following the existing security_overlay_warranted pattern)
- [Adversarial] Both Story 4 and Story 6 modify the Autonomous Resolution Loop in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md — coordinate insertion points to avoid shared-state conflicts. Story 6 should execute after Story 4 so the ratchet structure is stable before adding the mid-loop check

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
